channel.title: Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: Building the Beta
item.description: Lecture presentation on non-pipelined Beta implementation.

item.title: Cache Issues
item.description: Lecture presentation on cache design issues, associativity, replacement strategy, and performance analysis.

item.title: CMOS Technology
item.description: Lecture presentation on CMOS technology, gate design, and timing.

item.title: Computer Architecture: Exciting Times Ahead!
item.description: Lecture presentation on the future of computer architecture.

item.title: Cost/Performance Tradeoffs
item.description: Lecture presentation on cost/performance trade-offs and binary multiplication.

item.title: Designing an Instruction Set
item.description: Lecture presentation on the Beta instruction set architecture and compilation.

item.title: Devices & Interrupts
item.description: Lecture presentation on devices and interrupt handlers, preemptive interrupts, and real-time issues.

item.title: Finite State Machines
item.description: Lecture presentation on storage elements and synchronous finite state machines.

item.title: Interconnect & Communication
item.description: Lecture presentation on communication issues: busses, networks, and protocols.

item.title: Lecture Notes
item.description:
Abbreviations

CMOS = complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor
ALU = arithmetic logic unit
OS = operating system

LEC # TOPICS LECTURE NOTES
1 Course overview and mechanics, basics of information (PDF)
2 Digital abstraction, combinational logic, voltage-based encoding (PDF)
3 CMOS technology, gate design, timing (PDF)
4 Canonical forms; synthesis, simplification (PDF)
5 Sequential logic (PDF)
6 Storage elements, finite state machines (PDF)
7 Synchronization, metastability (PDF)

item.title: Machine Language, Assemblers, and Compilers
item.description: Lecture presentation on machine language programming issues, high level languages, optimization, assemblers, and compilers.

item.title: Parallel Processing
item.description: Lecture presentation on parallel processing, shared memory, cache coherence, and consistency criteria.

item.title: Pipeline Issues
item.description: Lecture presentation on pipeline issues: delay slots, annulment, and exceptions.

item.title: Pipelining
item.description: Lecture presentation on pipelining, throughput, and latency.

item.title: Pipelining the Beta
item.description: Lecture presentation on pipelined Beta implementation and bypassing.

item.title: Processes, Synchronization, & Deadlock
item.description: Lecture presentation on communicating processes, semaphores, synchronization, atomicity, and deadlock.

item.title: Programmability
item.description: Lecture presentation on models of computation and programmable architectures.

item.title: Sequential Logic
item.description: Lecture presentation on sequential logic, digital state, storage, and sequential circuits.

item.title: Stacks and Procedures
item.description: Lecture presentation on stacks and procedures.

item.title: Synchronization, Metastability and Arbitration
item.description: Lecture presentation on synchronization and metastability, and arbitration.

item.title: Synthesis of Combinational Logic
item.description: Lecture presentation on canonical forms, synthesis, and simplification.

item.title: The Digital Abstraction
item.description: Lecture presentation on digital abstraction, combinational logic, and voltage-based encoding.

item.title: The Memory Hierarchy
item.description: Lecture presentation on multilevel memories, locality, performance, and caches.

item.title: Virtual Machines
item.description: Lecture presentation on virtual machines: timesharing, OS kernels, and supervisor calls.

item.title: Virtual Memory
item.description: Lecture presentation on virtual memory: mapping, protection, and contexts.

item.title: Welcome to 6.004!
item.description: Lecture presentation with a course overview and introduction to computation structures.
